data.title: MarketAxess reports Q4 mixed results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• MarketAxess press release (NASDAQ:MKTX): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.73 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $202.4M (+2.6% Y/Y) misses by $0.25M.

  • Guidance: For the full year 2025, the company is providing the following guidance which is based on foreign exchange rates as of December 31, 2024:

    • Services revenue (combined Information Services, Post-Trade Services and Technology Services) is expected to grow in the mid-single digits (percent).
    • Expenses are expected to be in the range of $505.0 million to $525.0 million. Based on the midpoint of the stated guidance range, total expenses are expected to increase by approximately 8%.
    • The effective tax rate is expected to be between 23.5% and 24.5%, assuming no material changes in applicable tax laws.
    • Capital expenditures to support new protocols, products and trading platform enhancements are expected to be in the range of $65.0 million to $70.0 million.

data.title: Haemonetics reports mixed Q3 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Haemonetics press release (NYSE:HAE): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.19 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $348.5M (+3.7% Y/Y) misses by $3.88M.
  • Organic revenue for the third quarter of fiscal 2025 decreased 0.3% compared with the same period of fiscal 2024.
  • Cash flow from operating activities was $43.8 million and free cash flow was $29.6 million during the third quarter of fiscal 2025, compared with operating cash outflow of $0.5 million and free cash outflow of $22.5 million, respectively, in the same period of fiscal 2024.
  • Outlook: Adj EPS of (prior $4.45-$4.75) vs $4.59 consensus.

data.title: Linde Non-GAAP EPS of $3.97 beats by $0.05, revenue of $8.3B misses by $110M
data.description:
• Linde press release (NASDAQ:LIN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.97 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $8.3B (flat Y/Y) misses by $110M.

  • 2025 Guidance

    • First-quarter 2025 adjusted EPS guidance $3.85 - $3.95, represents 7%-9% growth ex. FX

    • Full-year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance $16.15 - $16.55 vs. $16.82 consensus, represents 8%-11% growth ex. FX

data.title: Xcel Energy misses Q4 top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Xcel Energy press release (NASDAQ:XEL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.81 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $3.12B (-9.3% Y/Y) misses by $650M.
  • Xcel Energy (XEL) reaffirms 2025 EPS guidance of $3.75 to $3.85 per share vs. $3.82 consensus.

data.title: Embecta declares $0.15 dividend
data.description:
• Embecta (NASDAQ:EMBC) declares $0.15/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.3%
  • Payable March 14; for shareholders of record Feb. 28; ex-div Feb. 28.
  • See EMBC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Hilton Worldwide beats Q4 bottom-line estimates, provides outlook
data.description:
• Hilton Worldwide press release (NYSE:HLT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.76 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $2.78B (+6.5% Y/Y) in-line.

  • Full Year 2025

    • System-wide comparable RevPAR, on a currency neutral basis, is projected to increase between 2.0 percent and 3.0 percent compared to 2024.
    • Diluted EPS is projected to be between $7.45 and $7.56.
    • Diluted EPS, adjusted for special items, is projected to be between $7.71 and $7.82.
    • Net income is projected to be between $1,829 million and $1,858 million.
    • Adjusted EBITDA is projected to be between $3,700 million and $3,740 million.
    • Contract acquisition costs and capital expenditures, excluding amounts reimbursed by third parties, are projected to be between $250 million and $300 million.
    • Capital return is projected to be approximately $3.3 billion.
    • General and administrative expenses are projected to be between $420 million and $430 million.
    • Net unit growth is projected to be between 6.0 percent and 7.0 percent.

    First Quarter 2025

    • System-wide comparable RevPAR, on a currency neutral basis, is projected to increase between 2.5 percent and 3.5 percent compared to the first quarter of 2024.
    • Diluted EPS is projected to be between $1.52 and $1.58.
    • Diluted EPS, adjusted for special items, is projected to be between $1.57 and $1.63.
    • Net income is projected to be between $373 million and $388 million.
    • Adjusted EBITDA is projected to be between $770 million and $790 million.

data.title: Commodities sector at 26-month high, UBS lifts gold price target to $3,000/oz
data.description:
UBS lifted its gold price target to $3,000/oz over the next 12 months, as bullion reached the Bank's long-held forecast of $2,850/oz, while broad strength, led by precious metals and agriculture, has driven a key commodities index to a 26-month-high.

“The global gold rush continues, with the yellow metal again outshining other asset classes year-to-date as escalating trade tensions and rising geopolitical uncertainties draw investors toward gold as a portfolio diversifier,” UBS analysts said.

The Bank believes "holding around 5% within a USD balanced portfolio is optimal from a diversification standpoint over the long term."

The increased target comes as gold demand reached a record high of 4,974 metric tons in 2024, according to the World Gold Council, which also indicated that central banks' net buying exceeded 1,000 metric tons for the third straight year.

Gold and silver (XAGUSD:CUR) have attracted most of the attention as they continue to rally higher, as investors seek protection, Ole Hansen, Saxo Bank's head of commodity said.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

In agriculture, coffee (KC1:COM) stands out, while the grains sector has started to recover following a near three-year slide.

"Key drivers behind our long-term bullish view on commodities include deglobalisation, defence, decarbonisation, de-dollarisation, and demographics," Hansen noted.

"Whether these strong gains can be maintained in the short term remains to be seen, but overall, we believe the direction of travel over time remains to the upside."

The current focus on tariffs and their potential negative impact on global growth has meanwhile been weighing mostly on the energy and industrial metals sectors.

However, the latter is seeing a divergence between copper and aluminium strength—two energy transition metals—and weakness in zinc and nickel, two metals used to galvanise iron and steel and for the production of stainless steel, Hansen added.

ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), (OTC:JJCTF)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)
